summary: Iteration 151 Cake Viewer full feature test complete. All 19 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend features verified: 5 tabs (Design/Intake/CutGuide/Portions/Templates), Mad Hatter tilted tiers, finish styles, toppers, client intake with auto-sizing, cut guide SVG, portion estimator with cost/margin, 4 starter templates, save/share/cutaway.

minor.issue: Returns 400 for guests > ~186 (max 4-tier capacity). This is expected behavior per code logic - suggests multiple cakes.

integration_issues.issue: Clipboard writeText fails in automation due to browser permissions - works in normal browser context
integration_issues.note: Browser security restriction, not a code bug

critical_code_review_comments: Backend cake_viewer.py (422 lines) well-structured with clear separation: Models, CRUD, Sizing Calculator, Cut Guide, Portion Estimator, Templates
Frontend CakeViewerPanel.tsx (777 lines) implements all 10 features with proper data-testid attributes for testing
Sizing calculator uses industry-standard serving chart (Wilton/professional) for accurate recommendations
Cut guide implements ring-cut math with proper slice calculations
Portion estimator correctly calculates filling costs + icing costs per tier with margin calculation
Mad Hatter template correctly includes 5 tiers with tilt_x/tilt_z/offset_x/offset_z values
MongoDB _id properly excluded from all API responses
test_credentials: No auth required for tested endpoints
seed_data_creation: Test sessions created with TEST_ prefix, cleaned up after tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter151 delivered 10 new Cake Viewer features: (1) Sessions CRUD with tilt/offset/finish/toppers/intake, (2) Sizing calculator for guest count → tier recommendations, (3) Cut guide with ring-cut math and SVG diagram, (4) Portion estimator with cost/margin breakdown, (5) 4 starter templates (classic_wedding, mad_hatter, birthday, naked_rustic), (6) Mad Hatter tilted/offset tiers, (7) Finish styles (buttercream/fondant/drip/mirror/naked/semi-naked), (8) Toppers library, (9) Client intake with BEO/delivery, (10) Cutaway cross-section view. All backend endpoints return 200 with proper data. Frontend panel opens via sidebar → Culinary Operations → Cake Viewer · 3D. Session cv-13d6cb0864 saved during testing.

backend_new: POST /api/cake-viewer/sessions with full iter151 payload (tilt, offset, finish, toppers, intake) ✓
GET /api/cake-viewer/portion-estimator?session_id=X - returns tiers breakdown, cost, margin ✓
GET /api/cake-viewer/templates/mad_hatter - returns 5 tiers with tilt values ✓
frontend_critical: Sidebar → Culinary Operations → Cake Viewer · 3D opens panel ✓
5 tabs visible: Design | Client Intake | Cut Guide | Portions & Cost | Templates ✓
Design tab: 3 default tiers with color pickers, finish dropdown, Mad Hatter toggle, Fillings toggle ✓
Client Intake tab: fields for name/BEO/event/guest count trigger sizing recommendation ✓
Sizing recommendation displays: 12"+16" = 156 servings for 150 guests ✓
Cut Guide tab: SVG diagram renders with rings and slice lines ✓
Cut Guide shows total slices (64) and step-by-step instructions ✓
Portions & Cost tab: shows 'Save first' message before save ✓
Portions & Cost tab: shows servings (92), cost ($252.36), per-tier breakdown after save ✓
Templates tab: 4 templates listed (classic_wedding, mad_hatter, birthday, naked_rustic) ✓
Mad Hatter template loads 5 tilted colorful tiers into 3D canvas ✓
Save button persists session, shows toast 'Saved · cv-xxx' ✓
URL hash updates with session ID ✓
Cutaway toggle slices tiers to reveal internal filling layers ✓

observations.sizing_calculator: Max single-unit cake capacity is ~186 servings (4-tier: 4"+8"+12"+16"). For larger events, API correctly suggests multiple cakes.
observations.cut_guide_math: Uses ring-cut method: outer ring 1" from edge, radial slices, repeat inward, center pie cut
observations.portion_estimator: Calculates filling cost + icing cost per tier. Icing costs: buttercream $0.25/svg, fondant $0.60, mirror $0.90
observations.mad_hatter_template: 5 tiers with varying tilt_x (-0.15 to 0.2), tilt_z (-0.12 to 0.15), offset_x (-0.2 to 0.18)
observations.cutaway_view: Uses thetaStart=π/4, thetaLength=1.5π for 270° visible, 90° cut revealing internal layers
